definition.description: A type of synesthesia where sound involuntarily evokes color, shape, and movement experiences. The synesthetic color supplements but does not obscure normal auditory perception.
definition.origin: First documented by Georg Tobias Ludwig Sachs in 1812; term 'colored hearing' first appeared in 1881 review of Bleuler & Lehmann's work

cross_activation_theory.core_idea: Adjacent brain regions (e.g., auditory cortex V4 for color) have increased connectivity due to reduced pruning during childhood
cross_activation_theory.projector_type: Fusiform gyrus - perceives photisms in external space
cross_activation_theory.associator_type: Angular gyrus - perceives photisms in mind's eye
cross_activation_theory.problem: Synesthesia should be present from birth but only evident mid-childhood

disinhibited_feedback_model.core_idea: No increased connectivity; instead, decreased inhibition in normal brain networks allows cross-activation
disinhibited_feedback_model.evidence: Can be acquired via retino-cortical damage, sensory deprivation, meditation, chemical agents
disinhibited_feedback_model.mechanism: Feedforward activity from inducer pathway activates convergent neurons; feedback propagates down concurrent pathway

individual_variance.idiosyncratic_but_consistent: Color associations are unique per individual but stable over time

projectors_vs_associators.note: Terms can be misleading; experience is not purely sensory/perceptual for most

drug_induced.mechanism: Non-selective serotonin agonists; affect ongoing transmission streams rather than stimulus-induced activation

form_constants.note: All hallucinations consist of these shapes; atypical = variations

vs_congenital.consistency: Drug-induced is NOT consistent or automatic

franz_liszt.association: Asked orchestra to play 'in a Bluer Fashion'
franz_liszt.note: Synesthesia not a common term in his era; people thought he was tricking them

leonard_bernstein.behavior: Stopped orchestras when timbre changed, even if listeners couldn't tell

amy_beach.sensitivity: Upset when artists changed keys (changed intended emotion)

tori_amos.experience: Music as structures of light since early childhood

olivier_messiaen.influence: Color of musical keys influenced compositions

alexander_scriabin.controversy: Debatable whether genuine synesthete or associative/theosophical system
alexander_scriabin.clavier_a_lumieres: Keyboard with lights for 'Prometheus: The Poem of Fire'
alexander_scriabin.circle_of_fifths_spectral: When ordered by circle of fifths, colors form a spectrum — casts doubt on genuine chromesthesia
alexander_scriabin.friend_rimsky_korsakov: Also synesthete but DIFFERENT mappings; more neutral/spontaneous character

brain_regions.V4: Visual color processing area activated immediately after auditory perception
brain_regions.inferior_parietal_cortex: Binds real and synesthetic perceptions into unified experience
brain_regions.left_superior_temporal_sulcus: Integrates auditory and visual information; responds to congruent pairs

research_findings.synesthetes_vs_nonsynesthetes: Both match sounds to colors non-arbitrarily; suggests common underlying mechanism
research_findings.genetic: Runs in families but may skip generations; monozygotic twins can differ
research_findings.absolute_pitch: INCREASES local variance in color matching (unexpected)
research_findings.semantic_mediation: Some individuals associate based on note name/belief rather than absolute pitch

implications_for_mrnn.crossmodal_binding_is_not_arbitrary: There are universal tendencies (high pitch = light, low pitch = dark)
implications_for_mrnn.emotion_mediates: Emotional inflection influences color — aligns with Lindborg et al. 2015
implications_for_mrnn.idiosyncratic_but_consistent: Each individual has unique mappings but they are stable — this is LEARNING, not randomness
implications_for_mrnn.attention_matters: 59% report attention influences experience — attention mechanism is critical
implications_for_mrnn.disinhibition_model_suggests: Crossmodal associations exist in ALL brains; synesthetes just have less inhibition — your MRNN should model this as default behavior
implications_for_mrnn.composers_as_evidence: Musicians with chromesthesia create based on these mappings — your engine can generate music from color and vice versa
implications_for_mrnn.scriabin_circle_of_fifths: Spectral ordering suggests HARMONIC relationships map to COLOR relationships — this is your 64-gate → color bridge
